request.description:
Retrieves all todos from the API.

**Method & Path:** `GET {{base_url}}/todos`

This endpoint returns a complete list of todo objects. The `{{base_url}}` environment variable must be set in the active environment (e.g., `https://dummyjson.com`).

**Response**

Returns a JSON object containing a `todos` array. Each todo object includes the following fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `id` | integer | Unique identifier of the todo |
| `todo` | string | The todo task description |
| `completed` | boolean | Whether the todo has been completed |
| `userId` | integer | ID of the user the todo belongs to |

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "todos": [
    { "id": 1, "todo": "Do something nice for someone you care about", "completed": false, "userId": 152 },
    { "id": 2, "todo": "Memorize a poem", "completed": true, "userId": 13 }
  ]
}
```

## Todos By ID

Retrieves a single todo item by its ID from the API.

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
GET {{base_url}}/todos/{{user_id}}
```

### Path Variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `{{user_id}}` | The ID of the todo item to retrieve |

### Expected Response

Returns a JSON object representing the todo item with the following fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `userId` | integer | The ID of the user who owns the todo |
| `id` | integer | The unique ID of the todo item |
| `title` | string | The title/description of the todo |
| `completed` | boolean | Whether the todo has been completed |

### Example Response

```json
{
  "userId": 1,
  "id": 5,
  "title": "laboriosam mollitia et enim quasi adipisci quia provident illum",
  "completed": false
}
```

Creates a new todo item. Sends a POST request to the todos endpoint using the `{{base_url}}` and `{{post_path}}` variables.

**Request Body (JSON):**
- `userId` (integer): The ID of the user the todo belongs to.
- `id` (integer): The unique identifier for the new todo item.
- `title` (string): The title/description of the todo. Uses the `{{todo_title}}` variable.
- `completed` (boolean): The completion status of the todo. Set to `false` for a newly created item.

**Response:**
Returns the created todo object with a `201 Created` status code.

## Complete todos Modification by ID

This request **fully replaces** an existing todo item identified by its ID, using the HTTP **PUT** method. Unlike a partial update (PATCH), a PUT request replaces the entire resource with the provided data.

**Endpoint:** `PUT {{base_url}}/todos/{{todo_id}}`

### Variables
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `{{base_url}}` | The base URL of the API (defined in the active environment) |
| `{{todo_id}}` | The unique identifier of the todo item to be replaced |

### Request Body (JSON)
```json
{
  "userId": 56,
  "title": "Formation Postam Jour 2 Postman",
  "completed": true
}
```

### Body Fields
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `userId` | integer | The ID of the user who owns the todo item |
| `title` | string | The title/label of the todo item |
| `completed` | boolean | Indicates whether the todo item is completed (`true`) or not (`false`) |

### Expected Response
On success, the API returns the updated todo object with a `200 OK` status code.

## Partial todos Modification by ID

This request performs a **partial update** on a specific todo item using the HTTP `PATCH` method. Unlike `PUT`, `PATCH` allows you to update only the fields you provide, leaving the rest of the resource unchanged.

---

### Endpoint

```
PATCH {{base_url}}/todos/{{todo_id}}
```

### Variables

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `{{base_url}}` | The base URL of the API (defined in the active environment) |
| `{{todo_id}}` | The unique identifier of the todo item to be partially updated |

---

### Request Body (raw JSON)

```json
{
    "userId": 56,
    "title": "Formation Postam Jour 2 PostmanPostman finished",
    "completed": true
}
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `userId` | integer | The ID of the user who owns the todo item |
| `title` | string | The updated title/description of the todo item |
| `completed` | boolean | Indicates whether the todo item has been completed (`true`) or not (`false`) |

---

### Expected Response

**Status:** `200 OK`

The response body contains the updated todo object reflecting the changes sent in the request body:

```json
{
    "userId": 56,
    "id": <todo_id>,
    "title": "Formation Postam Jour 2 PostmanPostman finished",
    "completed": true
}
```

## Delete todos by ID

Deletes a specific todo item by its unique identifier.

**Method:** `DELETE`
**Endpoint:** `{{base_url}}/todos/{{todo_id}}`

### Path Variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `todo_id` | The unique ID of the todo item to delete |

### Response

Returns an empty object `{}` or a `200 OK` status upon successful deletion.

## All Users

Retrieves a list of all users available in the system.

**Method:** `GET`

> ⚠️ No URL has been configured for this request yet. Set the `base_url` environment variable and update the endpoint (e.g., `{{base_url}}/users`) before sending.

### Response

Expected to return a JSON array of user objects, each containing fields such as `id`, `name`, `username`, `email`, and `address`.

## Dummy Jason Token

Authenticates a user and retrieves a JWT (JSON Web Token) for use in subsequent authenticated requests.

**Method:** `POST`
**Endpoint:** `{{base_url}}/auth/login`

### Request Body

Send a JSON object with user credentials:

```json
{
  "username": "your_username",
  "password": "your_password"
}
```

### Response

Returns a JSON object containing the access token:

```json
{
  "token": "<jwt_token>"
}
```

Use the returned token as a `Bearer` token in the `Authorization` header for protected endpoints.

## Get Auth Me

Retrieves the profile information of the currently authenticated user.

**Method:** `GET`
**Endpoint:** `{{base_url}}/auth/me`

### Authentication

Requires a valid JWT token. Include it in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer <token>
```

### Response

Returns a JSON object with the authenticated user's profile details, such as `id`, `username`, `email`, and `role`.

Retrieves a list of all posts.

**Method:** GET  
**Endpoint:** `{{base_url}}/posts`

**Response:**
- **200 OK** – Returns a JSON object containing an array of post objects. Each post includes:
  - `id` – Unique identifier of the post
  - `title` – Title of the post
  - `body` – Full text content of the post
  - `tags` – Array of tags associated with the post
  - `reactions` – Object containing `likes` and `dislikes` counts
  - `views` – Number of views
  - `userId` – ID of the user who created the post

**Notes:**
- Uses the `{{base_url}}` environment variable as the base URL.
- No authentication or query parameters are required.

## Posts by ID

Retrieves a single post by its unique identifier.

**Method:** `GET`
**Endpoint:** `{{base_url}}/posts/{{post_id}}`

### Path Variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `post_id` | The unique ID of the post to retrieve |

### Response

Returns a JSON object representing the post with the specified ID, including fields such as `id`, `title`, `body`, and `userId`.

## Comments by Post ID

Retrieves all comments associated with a specific post.

**Method:** `GET`
**Endpoint:** `{{base_url}}/posts/{{post_id}}/comments`

### Path Variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `post_id` | The unique ID of the post whose comments are to be retrieved |

### Response

Returns a JSON array of comment objects for the given post. Each comment includes fields such as `id`, `postId`, `name`, `email`, and `body`.

body.raw:
{"userId": 5,
"title": "test Post Postman formation Nabil",
"body": "Nabil test post formation Postman ."
}

request.description:
## Create Post

Creates a new post resource.

**Method:** `POST`
**Endpoint:** `{{base_url}}/posts{{post_path}}`

### Path Variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `post_path` | Optional path suffix for the post endpoint |

### Request Body

Send a JSON object with the post data. Typical fields include:

```json
{
  "title": "Post title",
  "body": "Post content",
  "userId": 1
}
```

### Response

Returns the newly created post object, including the assigned `id`.

## Delete Post

Deletes a specific post by its unique identifier.

**Method:** `DELETE`
**Endpoint:** `{{base_url}}/posts/{{post_id}}`

### Path Variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `post_id` | The unique ID of the post to delete |

### Response

Returns an empty object `{}` or a `200 OK` status upon successful deletion.

body.raw:
{
    "id": 1,
    "title": "His fatther had always taught him",
    "body": "His father had always taught him not to ever think of himself as better than others. He'd tried to live by this motto. He never looked down on those who were less fortunate or who had less money than him. But the stupidity of the group of people he was talking to made him change his mind.",
    "tags": [
        "history",
        "american",
        "crime",
        "fiction"
    ],
    "reactions": {
        "likes": 192,
        "dislikes": 25
    },
    "views": 305,
    "userId": 121
}

request.description:
## Modify Post

Fully replaces an existing post with new data (full update).

**Method:** `PUT`
**Endpoint:** `{{base_url}}/posts/{{post_id}}`

### Path Variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `post_id` | The unique ID of the post to update |

### Request Body

Send a complete JSON object with all post fields:

```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "title": "Updated title",
  "body": "Updated content",
  "userId": 1
}
```

### Response

Returns the updated post object reflecting the new values.

## Partially Modify Post

Applies a partial update to an existing post, modifying only the specified fields.

**Method:** `PATCH`
**Endpoint:** `{{base_url}}/posts/{{post_id}}`

### Path Variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `post_id` | The unique ID of the post to partially update |

### Request Body

Send a JSON object containing only the fields you wish to update:

```json
{
  "title": "New title only"
}
```

### Response

Returns the updated post object with the modified fields applied.
